  "text": "The gRPC C++ codebase implements health checking and keepalive mechanisms across several subsystems:\n\n**Health Checking Service (src/cpp/server/health/)**\n- `src/cpp/server/health/default_health_check_service.h` / `.cc`: Defines `DefaultHealthCheckService` (the default server-side health service implementation) and its inner `HealthCheckServiceImpl` (a `Service` subclass with a `WatchReactor` for streaming Watch calls). This is the primary server-side health checking implementation.\n- `src/cpp/server/health/health_check_service.cc`: Implements `EnableDefaultHealthCheckService()` / `DefaultHealthCheckServiceEnabled()` — the global toggle for the default health service.\n- `src/cpp/server/health/health_check_service_server_builder_option.cc`: Implements `HealthCheckServiceServerBuilderOption`, which injects a `HealthCheckServiceInterface` into server channel arguments via `UpdateArguments()`.\n\n**Keepalive Timer Logic (src/core/ext/transport/chttp2/transport/)**\n- `src/core/ext/transport/chttp2/transport/internal.h`: Defines the `grpc_chttp2_keepalive_state` enum (WAITING, PINGING, DYING, DISABLED) and the keepalive fields (`keepalive_ping_timer_handle`, `keepalive_time`, `keepalive_timeout`, `keepalive_state`) embedded in the transport struct.\n- `src/core/ext/transport/chttp2/transport/chttp2_transport.cc`: Contains `init_keepalive_pings_if_enabled_locked()` (starts the keepalive timer machinery) and `send_keepalive_ping_locked()` (sends the keepalive ping, piggybacking on in-flight pings if possible).\n- `src/core/ext/transport/chttp2/transport/frame_ping.cc`: Handles received PING frames including keepalive-related ping processing.\n- `src/core/ext/transport/chttp2/transport/ping_callbacks.cc` / `.h`: `Chttp2PingCallbacks` — manages outstanding ping callbacks.\n- `src/core/ext/transport/chttp2/transport/ping_rate_policy.cc` / `.h`: `Chttp2PingRatePolicy` — client-side policy controlling how frequently pings may be sent (rate-limiting, max pings without data).\n\n**Server-Side Keepalive Enforcement Policy**\n- `src/core/ext/transport/chttp2/transport/ping_abuse_policy.cc` / `.h`: `Chttp2PingAbusePolicy` — the server-side keepalive enforcement policy. `ReceivedOnePing()` tracks received pings and returns `true` if the connection should be closed due to ping abuse (too frequent pings, configurable via `GRPC_ARG_HTTP2_MIN_RECV_PING_INTERVAL_WITHOUT_DATA_MS` and `GRPC_ARG_HTTP2_MAX_PING_STRIKES`).\n\n**Client-Side Health Checking Channel Filter**\n- `src/core/load_balancing/health_check_client.h` / `.cc`: Exposes `MakeHealthCheckWatcher()`, which wraps a subchannel connectivity-state watcher with health-check awareness. LB policies use this to register watchers that incorporate health check status.\n- `src/core/load_balancing/health_check_client_internal.h`: Defines `HealthProducer` (registered with a subchannel as a `DataProducerInterface`; creates a streaming `Health.Watch` RPC for each health check service name), `HealthProducer::HealthChecker` (per-service-name health check client), and `HealthProducer::ConnectivityWatcher` (the connectivity watcher that observes subchannel state changes and drives health check lifecycle)."
}

  "text": "The gRPC C++ codebase implements health checking and keepalive mechanisms across several subsystems:\n\n**Health Checking Service (src/cpp/server/health/)**\n- `src/cpp/server/health/default_health_check_service.h` / `.cc`: Defines `DefaultHealthCheckService` (the default server-side health service implementation) and its inner `HealthCheckServiceImpl` (a `Service` subclass with a `WatchReactor` for streaming Watch calls). This is the primary server-side health checking implementation.\n- `src/cpp/server/health/health_check_service.cc`: Implements `EnableDefaultHealthCheckService()` / `DefaultHealthCheckServiceEnabled()` — the global toggle for the default health service.\n- `src/cpp/server/health/health_check_service_server_builder_option.cc`: Implements `HealthCheckServiceServerBuilderOption`, which injects a `HealthCheckServiceInterface` into server channel arguments via `UpdateArguments()`.\n\n**Keepalive Timer Logic (src/core/ext/transport/chttp2/transport/)**\n- `src/core/ext/transport/chttp2/transport/internal.h`: Defines the `grpc_chttp2_keepalive_state` enum (WAITING, PINGING, DYING, DISABLED) and the keepalive fields (`keepalive_ping_timer_handle`, `keepalive_time`, `keepalive_timeout`, `keepalive_state`) embedded in the transport struct.\n- `src/core/ext/transport/chttp2/transport/chttp2_transport.cc`: Contains `init_keepalive_pings_if_enabled_locked()` (starts the keepalive timer machinery) and `send_keepalive_ping_locked()` (sends the keepalive ping, piggybacking on in-flight pings if possible).\n- `src/core/ext/transport/chttp2/transport/frame_ping.cc`: Handles received PING frames including keepalive-related ping processing.\n- `src/core/ext/transport/chttp2/transport/ping_callbacks.cc` / `.h`: `Chttp2PingCallbacks` — manages outstanding ping callbacks.\n- `src/core/ext/transport/chttp2/transport/ping_rate_policy.cc` / `.h`: `Chttp2PingRatePolicy` — client-side policy controlling how frequently pings may be sent (rate-limiting, max pings without data).\n\n**Server-Side Keepalive Enforcement Policy**\n- `src/core/ext/transport/chttp2/transport/ping_abuse_policy.cc` / `.h`: `Chttp2PingAbusePolicy` — the server-side keepalive enforcement policy. `ReceivedOnePing()` tracks received pings and returns `true` if the connection should be closed due to ping abuse (too frequent pings, configurable via `GRPC_ARG_HTTP2_MIN_RECV_PING_INTERVAL_WITHOUT_DATA_MS` and `GRPC_ARG_HTTP2_MAX_PING_STRIKES`).\n\n**Client-Side Health Checking Channel Filter**\n- `src/core/load_balancing/health_check_client.h` / `.cc`: Exposes `MakeHealthCheckWatcher()`, which wraps a subchannel connectivity-state watcher with health-check awareness. LB policies use this to register watchers that incorporate health check status.\n- `src/core/load_balancing/health_check_client_internal.h`: Defines `HealthProducer` (registered with a subchannel as a `DataProducerInterface`; creates a streaming `Health.Watch` RPC for each health check service name), `HealthProducer::HealthChecker` (per-service-name health check client), and `HealthProducer::ConnectivityWatcher` (the connectivity watcher that observes subchannel state changes and drives health check lifecycle)."
}
